jokes.text: So, um, Axl, how was school today? / Uh-huh. / Did you learn anything? Or perhaps participate in shenanigans? / Uh-huh.
jokes.setup: Parent trying to have meaningful conversation with distracted teenager
jokes.punchline: Kid gives identical disinterested responses to different questions
jokes.explanation: Classic parent-teen dynamic, well-executed through repetitive non-responses

jokes.text: The flerk goes 'ba-KOOK-a-zoodle.'
jokes.setup: Child absorbed in device making random sounds
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical onomatopoeia that sounds like a cartoon sound effect
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the random absurdity of mobile game sounds, very quotable

jokes.text: Then what's the point of eating?
jokes.setup: Parents ask kids to put away devices during dinner
jokes.punchline: Child genuinely can't conceive of eating without screen entertainment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant encapsulation of screen addiction in kids - economical and perfectly captures the mindset

jokes.text: They asked me to put away my phone. I said, 'No.'
jokes.setup: Parent asks what happened at school
jokes.punchline: Kid matter-of-factly admits to defying authority with no shame
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing complete disconnect between generations

jokes.text: He's on his phone 24 hours a day, yet he's never called me once. I'd kill for a butt dial.
jokes.setup: Parent complaining about son's phone usage
jokes.punchline: Would be grateful for even an accidental call
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic observation about modern parent-child relationships, highly relatable and quotable

jokes.text: What if I post one of those catchy dance videos? Hoo, ooh, ooh. Hoo, hoo, hoo-hoo. Hoo!
jokes.setup: Parent desperate to connect with kids suggests making viral content
jokes.punchline: Performs embarrassing dance sounds
jokes.explanation: Classic dad trying to be cool moment, relies on cringe but fits character

jokes.text: Husband, ya can't dance. / Sure I can. My doctor okayed it.
jokes.setup: Wife states obvious fact about husband's dancing ability
jokes.punchline: Husband treats dancing like a medical procedure requiring clearance
jokes.explanation: Unexpected medical angle makes this funnier than standard 'dad can't dance' joke

jokes.text: Oh, bother. I'll have to fetch him from some dam-ned alternate reality.
jokes.setup: Professor trying to get Cubert for bedtime
jokes.punchline: Casually refers to VR/gaming as alternate reality with mild annoyance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor Farnsworth moment - treats sci-fi concept as mundane annoyance

jokes.text: Oh, bother. He's in an alternate, alternate reality.
jokes.setup: Repetition of previous attempt to fetch Cubert
jokes.punchline: Problem has gotten even more complicated with nested realities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation callback showing the problem getting worse

jokes.text: Kids. Am I right? / Yes. / I thought so. / Amen, brother.
jokes.setup: Fry makes generic observation seeking validation
jokes.punchline: Gets enthusiastic agreement from other parents
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective parent solidarity moment, shows Fry finding his tribe

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! Go Righty! / I've had precisely enough of this.
jokes.setup: Leela cheering something called 'Righty' enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to being fed up
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's quick emotional shift and self-awareness

jokes.text: Ugh!
jokes.setup: Kids told to go play outside in beautiful world
jokes.punchline: Collective disgusted reaction to the concept
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction showing kids' horror at offline activities

jokes.text: Man, I can barely even control that pigeon. This world needs an update.
jokes.setup: Kid experiencing reality without screens
jokes.punchline: Treats real world like buggy software that needs patching
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern perspective on reality - treating real world like a game with control issues

jokes.text: I'm jonesin', guys. I needs my net! / Yo, I got gigs! Uploads, downloads... What do you need?
jokes.setup: Kid desperate for internet access
jokes.punchline: Other kid acts like drug dealer but selling internet services
jokes.explanation: Good parallel between drug dealing and internet addiction, well-executed concept

jokes.text: You got 5G? I need a nickel. / Man, I got 10G. You want me to cut it with some static? / Nah, just hit us up.
jokes.setup: Continuation of internet drug deal parody
jokes.punchline: Technical terms mixed with drug slang ('cut it with static')
jokes.explanation: Extends the drug dealer metaphor with technical jargon, solid execution

jokes.text: If you're currently using another device in addition to the one you're watching me on, then you're a sick, sick bastard. So pick up a third device and call me now.
jokes.setup: Bender addressing screen addiction directly to audience
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts his own advice by encouraging more device use
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor and perfect Bender characterization - pretends to help while being completely self-serving

jokes.text: Together, we'll get through this, just me, you, and your credit card. In fact, forget you! Phone sex also available.
jokes.setup: Bender pretending to offer support for addiction
jokes.punchline: Reveals purely mercenary motives and adds inappropriate service
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - escalates from fake sympathy to pure greed to inappropriate offer

jokes.text: Hey, check it out! Bet I can get it up to 150. / Oh yeah? 170! Eat my beats! / Stop video-gaming your heart rates!
jokes.setup: Adults think kids are safe from devices in hospital
jokes.punchline: Kids turn their heart monitors into competitive games
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion showing kids will gamify anything, even medical equipment

jokes.text: Come on, fellow parents. Let's go back in time and not have kids. / Oh, yeah! / You said it.
jokes.setup: Parents frustrated with children's behavior
jokes.punchline: Suggests using time travel to avoid parenthood entirely
jokes.explanation: Dark parenting humor with sci-fi twist, relatable frustration taken to extreme

jokes.text: And to be clear, you are the wean-ees. I am the wean-er.
jokes.setup: Zapp explaining his screen addiction program
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily clarifies roles with made-up terms
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp verbose explanation of obvious concept, fits character perfectly

jokes.text: 'Why,' you ask, 'is the DOOP's rearest admiral running a children's camp?' / I didn't hear anybody ask that.
jokes.setup: Zapp anticipating question about his qualifications
jokes.punchline: Kid deflates his pompous setup by pointing out no one asked
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Zapp's ego, kid's honesty cuts through his pretension

jokes.text: In the old days, recruits came in looking like me. Kif, tighten my girdle so they can see what I look like.
jokes.setup: Zapp boasting about his physique and past recruits
jokes.punchline: Needs girdle tightened to look good, undermining his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment - vanity undermined by reality, girdle detail is great

jokes.text: And voila, I stand before you, an adonis.
jokes.setup: Zapp presenting his 'improved' physique after girdle adjustment
jokes.punchline: Grandiose self-description despite clearly not being an Adonis
jokes.explanation: Standard Zapp delusion of grandeur, works for character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Here! We got one more loser. / Hey! What'd I do? Nothin'! / You're as bad as everyone else, only worse.
jokes.setup: Bender protests being called a loser
jokes.punchline: Zapp's illogical explanation that he's worse than everyone for no reason
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp arbitrariness and Bender's innocent protest, solid character moment

jokes.text: Bite my shiny metal ass!
jokes.setup: Emotional goodbye scene with parents saying loving farewells
jokes.punchline: Bender's trademark crude farewell breaks the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender catchphrase in context, breaks sweet moment predictably but effectively

jokes.text: So, LaBarbara, with no kid around, it's the perfect chance for-- / Ladies' Night! / Don't wait up, Hermes.
jokes.setup: Hermes suggests romantic opportunity with kid away
jokes.punchline: Wife uses freedom to go out with friends instead
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection about couples' different priorities when kid-free

jokes.text: Ah, who needs them anyway? We can have our own Ladies' Night. Right, men? / Yes, we can! / We need it! / I call designated driver!
jokes.setup: Men left behind by wives going to Ladies' Night
jokes.punchline: Decide to have their own 'Ladies' Night' with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Men embracing feminine activity name, shows they just want to party too

jokes.text: Nevertheless, I shall hoist your devices high atop this naturally occurring stripper pole for one reason and one reason only, to taunt you. / It's working.
jokes.setup: Zapp explains his cruel but effective psychological torture method
jokes.punchline: Kid admits the taunting is successful
jokes.explanation: Good psychological torture humor, Zapp's honesty about cruelty and its effectiveness

jokes.text: I have built entire mobile game cities out of candy, but I can't put together one stupid tent?!
jokes.setup: Kid struggling with basic camping task
jokes.punchline: Contrasts complex digital achievements with simple physical failure
jokes.explanation: Good modern irony about digital vs physical skills, relatable generational gap

jokes.text: How are we supposed to do this if we can't watch a video on how to do this?
jokes.setup: Kids struggling with basic tasks
jokes.punchline: Genuinely can't conceive of learning without YouTube tutorials
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of modern learning dependency, highly relatable and quotable

jokes.text: Get it? / Oh, that's as cute as when Dwight said the same thing. / Hilarious.
jokes.setup: Woman shares story thinking it's unique and funny
jokes.punchline: Other woman reveals it's identical to someone else's story, delivered with sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Standard deflation of parent's pride in child's 'unique' behavior

jokes.text: How about if anyone mentions kids, they have to drink? / Sure! / Bring it on! / This is fun. I haven't been out day drinking since I had kids. Oops!
jokes.setup: Women create drinking game to avoid talking about kids
jokes.punchline: Immediately violates own rule while explaining why the game is fun
jokes.explanation: Good immediate failure of self-imposed rule, shows how hard it is to not think about kids

jokes.text: Ha! You look like Dwight after I give him goat liver oil.
jokes.setup: Woman drinking due to rule violation
jokes.punchline: Compares friend's expression to child's reaction to gross medicine
jokes.explanation: Decent comparison but not particularly inspired, keeps kid-mention theme going

jokes.text: Kids, kids, kids.
jokes.setup: Drinking game continues with more violations
jokes.punchline: Woman just gives up and says 'kids' multiple times
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of the drinking game failure, economical but not clever

jokes.text: Ugh. God forbid the driver should get a morsel of fresh air.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg criticized for not watching the road while driving
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically defends his right to fresh air while being the driver
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg self-pity and missing the point about driving safety

jokes.text: Right in my a-hole. / Antenna hole!
jokes.setup: Bender appears to use crude language about injury
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he means his antenna hole, not what audience assumed
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection using Bender's robot anatomy for clean version of crude joke

jokes.text: I just wish I had my phone so I could film this for lumberjack fails.
jokes.setup: Kid experiencing real physical comedy situation
jokes.punchline: Still thinks in terms of content creation rather than just experiencing moment
jokes.explanation: Great observation about modern inability to experience moments without documenting them

jokes.text: But sometimes being a quitter works, too.
jokes.setup: Zapp giving motivational speech about persistence in fire-starting
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives contradictory advice about quitting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp anti-leadership moment, undermines his own motivational message

jokes.text: I prefer cold stew anyway. Cold, raw stew.
jokes.setup: Zapp failing at basic camping tasks
jokes.punchline: Makes increasingly worse food sound appealing through denial
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp rationalization of failure, escalation makes it funnier

jokes.text: How do I look? / Sporky.
jokes.setup: Zapp asking for validation of his appearance
jokes.punchline: Kid gives brutally honest one-word assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and brutal honesty, 'sporky' is a great insult word

jokes.text: It's got the talons of an eagle, the fangs of a viper, the feathers of a pillow, and the angry red buttocks of an infected baboon.
jokes.setup: Zapp describing the mythical snipe creature
jokes.punchline: Starts with scary animal parts, includes mundane pillow feathers, ends with gross baboon detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation, pillow feathers in the middle is perfect bathos, baboon buttocks is memorable gross-out

jokes.text: Look, sunrise! / Then the hunt has begun. I'll be getting some shuteye.
jokes.setup: Zapp said they'd hunt at dawn, sunrise arrives
jokes.punchline: Zapp decides to sleep now that it's time to start
jokes.explanation: Classic Zapp leadership failure, abandons responsibility at crucial moment

jokes.text: And I'm a 12-year-old kid.
jokes.setup: Kids discussing whether snipes exist
jokes.punchline: Kid uses his age as credential for knowing about creatures
jokes.explanation: Cute logic that young age equals creature expertise, mild but charming

jokes.text: Man, the sun is sunning.
jokes.setup: Kid experiencing natural environment
jokes.punchline: Makes up verb form to describe sun's activity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective neologism, captures kid trying to describe natural phenomena

jokes.text: He's getting internet from the sun! This star must be a relay on the stellar backbone.
jokes.setup: Bender somehow getting connectivity in remote location
jokes.punchline: Kid creates elaborate tech explanation for impossible situation
jokes.explanation: 'Stellar backbone' is a great tech pun, good sci-fi comedy concept

jokes.text: Ooh, I'm a hotspot-bot!
jokes.setup: Kids want to use Bender's internet connection
jokes.punchline: Bender enjoys being useful as Wi-Fi hotspot
jokes.explanation: Cute portmanteau and Bender enjoys being technologically useful for once

jokes.text: You mean 'assword.' / 'Assword1'? / That's not a very secure assword.
jokes.setup: Kids trying to guess Bender's password
jokes.punchline: Bender calls it 'assword,' turns out to be predictably insecure
jokes.explanation: Good Bender wordplay and commentary on poor password security

jokes.text: Looking up 'snipes' on Snopes... and... Debunked! No such thing. / Whew. Well, that's a relief. 'Cause otherwise, I'd have screamed my head off when I saw that snipe.
jokes.setup: Kid researches snipes online, finds they don't exist, feels relieved
jokes.punchline: Reveals there's actually a snipe right there
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and timing, classic horror movie setup with good payoff

jokes.text: Fine. Fine. I'll increase my driving.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg criticized for going wrong way on one-way street
jokes.punchline: Responds by promising to 'increase' his driving rather than fix direction
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg misunderstanding of the problem, treats driving like volume

jokes.text: Fine. Fine. I'll increase my driving.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg driving wrong way on one-way street
jokes.punchline: Treating driving quality like a quantifiable skill to increase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg logic - treating driving like a video game stat to level up

jokes.text: Now you're going the wrong way on a two-way street.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg 'increased his driving' after wrong way on one-way street
jokes.punchline: Somehow made situation worse on easier street type
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Zoidberg made easier situation worse

jokes.text: Damn, this cabinet was three days from being used to store retirement paperwork.
jokes.setup: Car crashes into police station destroying property
jokes.punchline: Officer's oddly specific and mundane concern about cabinet timing
jokes.explanation: Good absurd specificity about bureaucratic concerns in crisis

jokes.text: According to Wikipedia snipes do exist! / That was me. I just updated it!
jokes.setup: Kid finds contradictory information about snipes online
jokes.punchline: Other kid reveals he just edited Wikipedia himself
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on Wikipedia reliability and kids' digital nativity

jokes.text: Uh, we call it a chicken.
jokes.setup: Kids being attacked by what they think is mythical snipe
jokes.punchline: Hippie casually reveals it's just a regular chicken
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of monster threat, great economy and timing

jokes.text: Brother? Ew! It's just a figure of speech, m'kay? We're cousins.
jokes.setup: Hippie calls partner 'brother,' character objects
jokes.punchline: Clarifies they're cousins as if that's better
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about relationship, implies incest while denying it

jokes.text: Generations have continued their dream through a careful program of selective inbreeding. / Not that careful.
jokes.setup: Hippie proudly describes 'careful' selective breeding program
jokes.punchline: Partner undermines claim about carefulness
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor deflation, perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: And then you kill 'em? / Nuh-uh, they die of fright, dig?
jokes.setup: Kid asks if they kill the snipes after catching them
jokes.punchline: Hippie claims moral high ground while describing same outcome
jokes.explanation: Good hippie hypocrisy about violence, death by terror is worse than killing

jokes.text: Even fun? What do you do for fun? / Fun? What is this 'fun'? / Like, what do you like to watch? / Plants growing! / Go corn! / You got this, radish!
jokes.setup: Kid asks what hippies do for entertainment
jokes.punchline: Hippies enthusiastically cheer on vegetables like sports
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist concept of vegetables as sports entertainment, very quotable cheering

jokes.text: This is what we do on Earth. / Whoa! Don't look! It'll take your souls. / It's just solitaire. / Witchcraft and dark science!
jokes.setup: Kid shows hippies something normal from Earth
jokes.punchline: Hippies react with terror to mundane card game
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash humor, solitaire as 'dark science' is a nice touch

jokes.text: Don't listen to the chubby wizard. / I have a metabolic condition.
jokes.setup: Hippie dismisses kid as 'chubby wizard'
jokes.punchline: Kid's defensive medical explanation
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke with medical excuse, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Get it off! Get it off! It's a mole!
jokes.setup: Action sequence with hippies attacking
jokes.punchline: Someone panicking about tiny harmless creature during battle
jokes.explanation: Standard action comedy bit, fear of harmless creature during danger

jokes.text: In the name of peace, aim for the head!
jokes.setup: Hippies engaged in violent battle
jokes.punchline: Invoking peace while giving lethal combat instructions
jokes.explanation: Excellent hippie hypocrisy, perfect contradiction between peaceful ideals and violent action

jokes.text: Two tin cans on a string? / It's the latest in affordable tech. The battery life is excellent, and it smells like cream of mushroom soup.
jokes.setup: Someone questions Fry's primitive communication device
jokes.punchline: Fry enthusiastically markets its 'features' including random smell
jokes.explanation: Good Fry optimism about terrible technology, mushroom soup smell is odd specific detail

jokes.text: Really? I never gave him my number.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests Santa might call Fry
jokes.punchline: Fry takes it literally and realizes the logical problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry literal-mindedness about fantasy figure, simple but effective

jokes.text: We're sorry. This call cannot be completed. Please hang up and worry.
jokes.setup: Parent desperately trying to reach child during emergency
jokes.punchline: Phone system's advice to 'hang up and worry' instead of helpful action
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy subversion of helpful phone messages, highly quotable

jokes.text: Sounds like the emergency is your relationship.
jokes.setup: Parent worried about emergency call from son
jokes.punchline: Onlooker suggests real emergency is their bad relationship
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate observation about parent-child disconnect

jokes.text: It's a total eclipse of the internet!
jokes.setup: Solar eclipse causing internet outage
jokes.punchline: References famous song 'Total Eclipse of the Heart' but about internet
jokes.explanation: Good song reference pun, captures modern dependence on internet connectivity

jokes.text: That's 'cause it needs to connect to a server in Des Moines, Iowa. / Des Moines, Iowa!
jokes.setup: Zapp's weapons don't work
jokes.punchline: Requires internet connection to specific mundane location to function
jokes.explanation: Good satire of always-online technology dependency, Des Moines specificity is funny

jokes.text: Oh, why did I pick today to start my life of crime?
jokes.setup: Character unable to escape due to technology failure
jokes.punchline: Bad timing complaint about beginning criminal career
jokes.explanation: Standard timing complaint joke, nothing particularly clever

jokes.text: Sweet skink of the clink!
jokes.setup: Character expressing surprise at finding old technology
jokes.punchline: Prison-themed exclamation with alliterative animal reference
jokes.explanation: Creative prison-themed exclamation, fits criminal character's speech pattern

jokes.text: Not since suicide booths started takin' Venmo.
jokes.setup: Explaining why nobody has coins anymore
jokes.punchline: Suicide booths accepting modern payment methods
jokes.explanation: Dark Futurama worldbuilding joke, suicide booths accepting Venmo is perfectly dystopian

jokes.text: All I wanted was to never speak to him again. And now I'll never speak to him again!
jokes.setup: Parent realizing their wish about not talking to their child
jokes.punchline: Gets exactly what they wanted in worst possible way
jokes.explanation: Excellent dramatic irony about getting what you wish for, good emotional weight

jokes.text: I'm leaving you jokers behind when I bust out of this joint, see?
jokes.setup: Group needs to work together to solve phone problem
jokes.punchline: Criminal robot declares selfish escape plan with classic gangster talk
jokes.explanation: Standard criminal character betrayal moment with period-appropriate slang

jokes.text: If I'm honest, here we've had a mild summer. / Sorcery!
jokes.setup: Hippie making normal small talk about weather
jokes.punchline: Reacts with terror to his own mundane statement
jokes.explanation: Good hippie character moment, fears his own rational thought

jokes.text: We call it content. / We will call it 'God.'
jokes.setup: Kid explains modern digital entertainment
jokes.punchline: Hippies decide to worship it as deity
jokes.explanation: Excellent commentary on screen worship in modern society, hippies' instant conversion is perfect

jokes.text: Dude.
jokes.setup: Witnessing his father being killed by giant snipe
jokes.punchline: Completely understated reaction to traumatic event
jokes.explanation: Perfect millennial understatement to extreme trauma, excellent economy and timing

jokes.text: Are... you... okay? / Oh, he's not replying. Maybe I should text him six more times.
jokes.setup: Parent texting worried message to child
jokes.punchline: Immediately considers sending multiple follow-up texts
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern parent texting anxiety behavior

jokes.text: Yes. We just had a beautiful conversation.
jokes.setup: Parent asks if Kif talked to their son
jokes.punchline: Kif's positive description after we saw the terrifying emergency call
jokes.explanation: Good ironic callback to the traumatic phone call, Kif's optimism contrasts with reality
